title: adenophora gmelinii var. coronopifolia
intro: Adenophora gmelinii var. coronopifolia, also known as Adenophorae, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and relieving menstrual cramps, as well as possessing antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. In traditional herbal preparations, Adenophorae is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and supplements.
